flashcards.front: What is an Iteration in the SDLC?
flashcards.back: A single development cycle where a team completes a set of tasks (plan, design, implement, test) to produce a working subset of the final product.
The sum of all the product features completed and integrated through the iterations so far. It is a stable, executable, and tested version of the product.

flashcards.front: Name one key advantage of iterative development.
flashcards.back: Early and continuous risk management, as high-risk areas are tackled in early iterations. OR Early customer feedback leading to a more aligned final product.

flashcards.front: What are the four quadrants of the Spiral Model?
flashcards.back: 1. Determine objectives, alternatives, and constraints. 2. Evaluate alternatives, identify and resolve risks. 3. Develop and verify the next-level product. 4. Plan the next phases.

flashcards.front: How does the Iterative model promote flexibility?
flashcards.back: Changing requirements can be incorporated at the beginning of the next iteration based on feedback from the previous increment, making the process adaptable.
